mechanism_narrative: A persistently low and stable federal funds rate keeps the equity risk premium compressed relative to bond yields, sustaining a positive drift in equity valuations. Continuous low FEDFUNDS levels below prior-cycle norms support forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon regardless of short-term news flow.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20-day SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or returns during low-rate periods are not statistically distinguishable from high-rate periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points from its trailing 12-month low, this early recession signal (Sahm-rule-like threshold) triggers risk-off repricing across equities, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter as markets price rising layoff risk and earnings deceleration.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% near cycle lows as of 2017-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances historically, or the signal fails to precede any NBER-dated recession within 12 months in more than half of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in PAYEMS month-over-month growth and INDPRO month-over-month growth signals synchronized labor-market and industrial expansion, reinforcing corporate earnings momentum. Neither series alone reliably predicts returns, but their joint positive co-movement produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month via reinforced growth expectations.
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS+INDPRO acceleration signal shows no incremental predictive power over either series individually (interaction coefficient not significant at p<0.10) across a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During housing-market expansion regimes, rising HOUST housing starts signal robust consumer confidence and durable-goods demand, feeding through construction employment and consumption multipliers into corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon. This relationship is strongest when housing starts trend above their prior 12-month average.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1090K units, near post-crisis highs as of 2017-01
failure_condition: During periods when HOUST trends above its 12-month moving average, forward 60-day SPX returns are not significantly higher than the unconditional average (t-test p > 0.05), or the relationship inverts during a housing expansion accompanied by monetary tightening.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in RSAFS retail sales reflects real-time consumer spending strength, a leading indicator of near-term corporate revenue surprises. Positive RSAFS momentum produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week as markets incorporate improving consumption signals ahead of earnings releases.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS monthly growth and forward 5-day SPX return remains below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, indicating no exploitable short-horizon signal.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation accelerates above a 2.5% threshold, markets anticipate faster Federal Reserve tightening, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month until inflation stabilizes.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 242.348 as of two months prior to decision date
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 2.5%, or FEDFUNDS fails to rise in the 6 months following such triggers in more than half of historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A Taylor-rule-style mismatch where FEDFUNDS remains historically low while UNRATE is simultaneously near multi-year lows signals an overheating labor market relative to policy stance, implying future rate-hike surprises. This joint condition produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as markets price accelerated tightening risk not evident from either series alone.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.54% and UNRATE at 4.7% as of late 2016/early 2017
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower during joint low-FEDFUNDS/low-UNRATE regimes compared to baseline periods (p > 0.05), or the policy tightening implied by the mismatch does not materialize within 12 months in more than half of episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In periods of decelerating GDPC1 quarterly growth combined with flattening INDPRO, industrial output weakness signals impending supply-side drag on corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following trading week as markets react to early signs of production slowdown before broader confirmation.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with decelerating output growth
failure_condition: Rank IC between combined GDPC1/INDPRO deceleration signal and forward 5-day SPX return stays below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following deceleration signals are not significantly negative (p > 0.05).
